---
title: "AMC-Taxonomy_Stats"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-02"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 5,
  out.width = "80%"
)


```

# Kraken2

Taxonomical Analysis

```{r taxonomy_analysis, results="hide"}

# load necessary packages
library(BiocManager)
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(microViz)
library(tidylog)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)
library(readxl)
library(viridis)
library(dunn.test)
library(microbiome)
library(pairwiseAdonis)
library(ape)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggnewscale)
library(scico)
library(grid)

ps_tax <- readRDS(url("https://github.com/grazicioletti/AMC-Calf_Nasopharyngeal/raw/main/data/ps_tax.RDS"))

```


## Decontam

```{r decontam_tax}

# Decontamination using decontam package
library(decontam)

ps3_tax <- ps_tax %>%
  ps_mutate(
    SampleBinary = if_else(str_detect(Trt, "NegativeControl"), true = "Control", false = "Sample")
  )

# Identify contaminants using prevalence method
sample_data(ps3_tax)$is.neg <- sample_data(ps3_tax)$SampleBinary == "Control"
contamdf.prev <- isContaminant(ps3_tax, method="prevalence", neg="is.neg", threshold = 0.8)

# Create phyloseq object of only contaminants in the controls
con_tax <- rownames(contamdf.prev[contamdf.prev$contaminant == "TRUE", ])
conps_tax <- prune_taxa(rownames(ps3_tax@tax_table) %in% con_tax, ps3_tax)
negps_tax <- subset_samples(conps_tax, is.neg == "TRUE")

# Extract list of contaminants
contams_tax <- rownames(contamdf.prev[contamdf.prev$contaminant == "TRUE", ])
invisible(contams_tax) # 6255
length(contams_tax)

# Remove contaminant sequences
noncontam_tax <- prune_taxa(!rownames(ps3_tax@tax_table) %in% contams_tax, ps3_tax)

# Filter controls
controls_tax <- ps3_tax %>%
  ps_filter(SampleBinary == "Control", .keep_all_taxa = TRUE)

controls_tax <- sample_data(controls_tax)$Project_ID 

# Remove all controls
noneg_tax <- ps_filter(noncontam_tax, !Project_ID %in% controls_tax)
invisible(factor(sample_data(noneg_tax)$Trt))

# Remove positive control
noneg_tax <- ps_filter(noneg_tax, Trt != "PositiveControl")
invisible(factor(sample_data(noneg_tax)$Trt))

# Prevalence-based filtering for negonly decontam
sort(phyloseq::sample_sums(noneg_tax))

x <- phyloseq::sample_sums(noneg_tax)

summary(x)

(noneg_tax <- phyloseq::subset_samples(noneg_tax, phyloseq::sample_sums(noneg_tax) > 2000))
(noneg_tax <- phyloseq::prune_taxa(phyloseq::taxa_sums(noneg_tax) > 0, noneg_tax))
noneg_tax <- noneg_tax %>% tax_filter(
  min_prevalence = 0.01,
  min_total_abundance = 0.0001)
  
# Display sample data
invisible(sample_data(noneg_tax))

# total % of reads/taxa removed
sum(contamdf.prev$contaminant) / nrow(contamdf.prev)

table(contamdf.prev$contaminant)


# fix labels and factor 
sample_data(noneg_tax)$Diet <- recode_factor(
  sample_data(noneg_tax)$Diet,
  "Ab" = "MR+A",
  "No" = "MR"
)

sample_data(noneg_tax)$Diet <- factor(
  sample_data(noneg_tax)$Diet,
  levels = c("MR+A","MR")
)

sample_data(noneg_tax)$Age_wk <- factor(
  sample_data(noneg_tax)$Age_wk,
  levels = c("1","3","5","7","9","11", "13", "15")
)

```

```{r qc-contam}

## ── Define longitudinal analysis window
## Early and later sampling weeks (3 and  15) showed elevated and unstable contaminant burden and introduced disproportionate structure in ordination and mixed models. These timepoints were therefore excluded from primary longitudinal analyses. 

## Week 1 and 9 wewe borderline but still retained because contaminant distributions overlapped with adjacent weeks and sampling representation was complete.

contam_prop <- psmelt(transform_sample_counts(ps3_tax, function(x) x / sum(x))) %>%
  mutate(is_contam = OTU %in% contams_tax) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Age_wk) %>%
  summarise(
    contam_fraction = sum(Abundance[is_contam], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

contam_prop$Age_wk <- factor(
  as.character(contam_prop$Age_wk),
  levels = c("1","3","5","7","9","11","13","15")
)

## Save QC summary for traceability
contam_qc_summary <- contam_prop %>%
  group_by(Age_wk) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_contam = median(contam_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_contam = mean(contam_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_contam = max(contam_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(contam_qc_summary)

ggplot(contam_prop, aes(Age_wk, contam_fraction)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "grey90", colour = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    x = "Age (weeks)",
    y = "Fraction contaminant reads"
  )

## ── Define final primary analysis window
analysis_weeks <- c("1", "5","7","9","11", "13")

excluded_timepoints <- ps_filter(
  noneg_tax,
  !as.character(Age_wk) %in% analysis_weeks
)

noneg_tax <- ps_filter(
  noneg_tax,
  as.character(Age_wk) %in% analysis_weeks
)

sample_data(noneg_tax)$Age_wk <- factor(
  as.character(sample_data(noneg_tax)$Age_wk),
  levels = analysis_weeks
)

nsamples(noneg_tax)
table(sample_data(noneg_tax)$Age_wk)

cat(
  "Weeks retained for primary longitudinal analysis:",
  paste(levels(sample_data(noneg_tax)$Age_wk), collapse = ", "),
  "\n"
)

``` 
<br>

## Alpha Diversity

```{r models}
## Alpha diversity — MODEL-BASED ANALYSIS

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(glmmTMB)
library(emmeans)

## prepare dataframe
sampdf <- sample_data(noneg_tax) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ID")

alpha <- estimate_richness(
  noneg_tax,
  measures = c("Shannon","Observed")
) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ID") %>%
  left_join(sampdf, by = "ID")

## remove extreme outliers (same rule)
detect_outliers <- function(df, metric){
  m <- mean(df[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE)
  s <- sd(df[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE)
  df %>%
    filter(.data[[metric]] < m-3*s |
           .data[[metric]] > m+3*s) %>%
    pull(ID)
}

outlier_ids <- unique(c(
  detect_outliers(alpha,"Shannon"),
  detect_outliers(alpha,"Observed")
))

alpha_clean <- alpha %>%
  filter(!ID %in% outlier_ids)

## factor structure
alpha_clean$Age_wk <- factor(
  alpha_clean$Age_wk,
  levels = c("1", "5","7","9","11", "13")
)

alpha_clean$Animal_ID <- factor(alpha_clean$Animal_ID)

library(simr)
simr::simrOptions(progress = FALSE)
simrOptions(progress = FALSE)

alpha_clean$Diet <- relevel(alpha_clean$Diet, ref = "MR")

## MIXED MODELS
lmm_shannon <- lmer(
  Shannon ~ Diet * Age_wk + (1 | Animal_ID),
  data = alpha_clean,
  REML = FALSE
)

lmm_observed <- glmmTMB(
  Observed ~ Diet * Age_wk + (1 | Animal_ID),
  family = nbinom2,
  data = alpha_clean
)

anova(lmm_shannon)
car::Anova(lmm_observed, type = "III")

## MODEL-ESTIMATED MEANS (emmeans)
emm_shannon <- emmeans(lmm_shannon, ~ Diet | Age_wk)

emm_observed <- emmeans(
  lmm_observed,
  ~ Diet | Age_wk,
  type = "response"
)

emm_shannon_df <- as.data.frame(emm_shannon)
emm_observed_df <- as.data.frame(emm_observed)

# Pairwise Diet contrasts at each Age_wk
contrast(emm_shannon, "pairwise", by = "Age_wk", adjust = "BH")
contrast(emm_observed, "pairwise", by = "Age_wk", adjust = "BH", type = "response")

## ===== POST-HOC DETECTABLE EFFECT SIZE (Shannon) =====
se_diet <- summary(
  contrast(emm_shannon, "pairwise", by = "Age_wk")
)$SE[1]

# approximate detectable difference (two-sided alpha 0.05)
detectable_effect <- 2 * se_diet

se_diet
detectable_effect

## ===== Detectable fold-change for Observed richness =====

coefs <- summary(lmm_observed)$coefficients$cond

se_obs <- coefs["DietMR+A","Std. Error"]

## approximate detectable log-effect
detectable_log <- 2 * se_obs

## convert to fold-change
detectable_ratio <- exp(detectable_log)

se_obs
detectable_ratio

# -------------- PLOTTING
treatment_colors <- c(
  "MR+A" = "#C48C51",
  "MR"   = "#5A763F"
)

## MODEL-BASED SHANNON PLOT
ggplot(emm_shannon_df,
       aes(x = Age_wk,
           y = emmean,
           fill = Diet)) +
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = position_dodge(0.8),
    width = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = emmean - SE,
      ymax = emmean + SE
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.8),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
  data = alpha_clean,
  aes(x = Age_wk,
      y = Shannon,
      group = Diet),   
  position = position_jitterdodge(
    jitter.width = 0.12,
    dodge.width = 1.0
  ),
  color = "black",
  size = 2,
  alpha = 0.6,
  inherit.aes = FALSE
) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = treatment_colors) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Age (weeks)",
    y = "Model-estimated Shannon diversity"
  )

## MODEL-BASED OBSERVED PLOT
ggplot(emm_observed_df,
       aes(x = Age_wk,
           y = response,
           fill = Diet)) +
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = position_dodge(0.8),
    width = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = response - SE,
      ymax = response + SE
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.8),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
  data = alpha_clean,
  aes(x = Age_wk,
      y = Observed,
      group = Diet),   
  position = position_jitterdodge(
    jitter.width = 0.12,
    dodge.width = 0.8
  ),
  color = "black",
  size = 2,
  alpha = 0.6,
  inherit.aes = FALSE
)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = treatment_colors) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Age (weeks)",
    y = "Model-estimated richness"
  )

``` 

<br>

<br><br>

## Beta Diversity

```{r beta_diversity}

## TAXONOMIC BETA DIVERSITY — Aitchison (Genus)

set.seed(12345)

## relative abundance transform
pst_all <- transform_sample_counts(noneg_tax, function(x) x / sum(x))

## CLR transform at Genus level
beta_tax <- pst_all %>%
  tax_transform(trans = "clr", rank = "Genus")

## Aitchison distance
psdist_tax <- phyloseq::distance(beta_tax, method = "euclidean")

## metadata
meta_tax <- data.frame(sample_data(beta_tax))

## -------------------------------
## beta dispersion
bd_tax <- betadisper(psdist_tax, meta_tax$Diet)
anova(bd_tax)
permutest(bd_tax, permutations = 999)

## PERMANOVA longitudinal
adonis_tax <- adonis2(
  psdist_tax ~ Age_wk + Diet + Diet:Age_wk,
  data = meta_tax,
  permutations = 10000,
  strata = meta_tax$Animal_ID
)

adonis_tax

## PCoA ordination
ord_tax <- ape::pcoa(psdist_tax)

scores_df <- as.data.frame(ord_tax$vectors[, 1:2])
colnames(scores_df) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

scores_df$SampleID <- rownames(scores_df)
meta_tax$SampleID <- rownames(meta_tax)

scores_df <- merge(scores_df, meta_tax, by = "SampleID")

## order age legend
scores_df$Age_wk <- factor(scores_df$Age_wk, levels = c("1", "5", "7", "9", "11", "13"))

## percent variance explained
var_exp <- round(ord_tax$values$Relative_eig[1:2] * 100, 1)

## palettes and shapes
age_palette <- setNames(
  scico::scico(length(levels(scores_df$Age_wk)), palette = "vikO"),
  levels(scores_df$Age_wk)
)

## IMPORTANT:
## use the Diet labels that actually exist in your metadata
table(scores_df$Diet)

## If your levels are MR / MR+A instead, use these instead:
shape_vals <- c("MR" = 17, "MR+A" = 16)
treatment_colors <- c(
  "MR+A" = "#C48C51",
  "MR"   = "#5A763F")

## final plot
p_tax <- ggplot(
  scores_df,
  aes(PC1, PC2, color = Age_wk, shape = Diet)
) +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0, show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_vals, name = "Diet") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = age_palette,
    name = "Age (weeks)",
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
      linetype = 0,  
      shape = 16,
      size = 3))
  ) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(color = Diet, group = Diet),
    level = 0.95,
    linewidth = 0.9,
    alpha = 0.9,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = treatment_colors, name = "Diet") +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PCoA1 (", var_exp[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PCoA2 (", var_exp[2], "%)")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.45, "cm"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 10, 5, 5),
    plot.title = element_blank()
  )

p_tax

```


``` {r taxa_abund-prev}

library(dplyr)
library(microbiome)

# Transform to relative abundance
rel_abund <- microbiome::transform(noneg_tax, "compositional")

tax <- as.matrix(tax_table(noneg_tax))
tax <- gsub("^[a-z]__", "", tax)
tax_table(noneg_tax) <- tax

# Extract abundance and taxonomy
otu_tab <- as.data.frame(otu_table(rel_abund))
tax_tab <- as.data.frame(tax_table(rel_abund))

# Add Genus to abundance matrix
otu_tab$Genus <- tax_tab$Genus

# Reshape into long format for accuracy
library(tidyr)
otu_long <- otu_tab %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("OTU") %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(OTU, Genus), names_to = "Sample", values_to = "RelAbundance")

# Compute mean relative abundance per Genus
mean_abund_genus <- otu_long %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(Mean_RelAbundance = mean(RelAbundance, na.rm = TRUE) * 100) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_RelAbundance))

# Get top 30
top30_genus <- head(mean_abund_genus, 30)

print(top30_genus, n=30)

```

```{r composition-genus}

# GLOBAL TOP-12 (shotgun feature mean relative abundance)
ps_rel <- microbiome::transform(noneg_tax, "compositional")

otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_rel))
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_rel))

otu_df$Genus <- as.character(tax_df$Genus)

otu_long <- otu_df %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Feature") %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(Feature, Genus),
               names_to = "Sample",
               values_to = "RelAbundance")

mean_abund_genus <- otu_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genus)) %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_RelAbundance = mean(RelAbundance),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_RelAbundance))

top10_genus <- mean_abund_genus %>% slice_head(n = 10)

top_taxa <- top10_genus$Genus

# PALETTE
custom_palette <- c(
    "#450C54", "#6D597A", "#24785E",
    "#90BE6D", "lightgoldenrod2", "#F36778", "#F92", 
    "#297777",  "#704",
    "#233445", "#B99926", "#4B3525"
)
     
# AGGREGATE TO GENUS FOR PLOTTING
ps_genus <- tax_glom(noneg_tax, "Genus")

tax_table(ps_genus)[, colnames(tax_table(ps_genus))] <-
  gsub("[a-z]__", "", tax_table(ps_genus)[, colnames(tax_table(ps_genus))])

ps_plot <- prune_taxa(
  as.character(tax_table(ps_genus)[,"Genus"]) %in% top_taxa,
  ps_genus
)

ps_plot <- microbiome::transform(ps_plot, "compositional")

sample_data(ps_plot)$Age_wk <- factor(
  sample_data(ps_plot)$Age_wk,
  levels = c("1", "5","7","9","11", "13")
)

# BUILD MEAN DATA
df <- psmelt(ps_plot)

mean_df <- df %>%
  group_by(Diet, Age_wk, Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    MeanRA = mean(Abundance),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ORDER TAXA (dominant at bottom)
taxa_order <- mean_df %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(globalMean = mean(MeanRA), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(globalMean)) %>%
  pull(Genus)

mean_df$Genus <- factor(
  mean_df$Genus,
  levels = rev(taxa_order)
)

# palette mapped bottom up
taxa_levels <- levels(mean_df$Genus)
pal <- custom_palette[seq_along(taxa_levels)]
names(pal) <- rev(taxa_levels)

# FINAL PLOT
ggplot(mean_df,
       aes(x = Age_wk,
           y = MeanRA,
           fill = Genus)) +
    geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = "stack",
    width = 0.975
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = pal,
    breaks = rev(taxa_levels),
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_pubclean(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = margin(3,3,3,3)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(
    ncol = 5,
    keywidth = 0.6,
    keyheight = 0.5
  )) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("1", "5","7","9","11", "13"),
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~Diet, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    y = "Relative abundance",
    x = "Age (weeks)"
  )

# relative abundance
ps_rel <- microbiome::transform(noneg_tax, "compositional")

# extract matrices
otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_rel))
tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_rel))

otu$Genus <- tax$Genus

# long format
otu_long <- otu %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Feature") %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(Feature, Genus),
               names_to = "Sample",
               values_to = "RelAbundance")

# genus summary
genus_table <- otu_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genus)) %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_RA = mean(RelAbundance) * 100,
    Prevalence = mean(RelAbundance > 0) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_RA)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 30)

print(genus_table, n =100)
  
```


``` {r reproducibility}

# SAVE REPRODUCIBILITY TABLES FOR GitHub — TAXONOMY PIPELINE

dir.create("taxonomic", showWarnings = FALSE)

# 1. Decontamination outputs
write.csv(contamdf.prev,
          "taxonomic/decontam_results.csv",
          row.names = TRUE)

writeLines(contams_tax,
           "taxonomic/list_of_contaminants.txt")
           
# 2. Power analysis
write.csv(
  data.frame(
    metric = c("Shannon", "Observed"),
    se_diet = c(se_diet, se_obs),
    detectable_difference = c(detectable_effect, NA),
    detectable_ratio = c(NA, detectable_ratio)
  ),
  "taxonomic/posthoc_sensitivity_summary.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 3. Alpha diversity datasets
write.csv(alpha,
          "taxonomic/alpha_raw.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(alpha_clean,
          "taxonomic/alpha_clean.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.table(outlier_ids,
            "taxonomic/alpha_outlier_ids.txt",
            row.names = FALSE,
            col.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

# 4. Mixed model outputs
saveRDS(lmm_shannon,
        "taxonomic/lmm_shannon_model.rds")

saveRDS(lmm_observed,
        "taxonomic/lmm_observed_model.rds")

capture.output(summary(lmm_shannon),
               file = "taxonomic/lmm_shannon_summary.txt")

capture.output(summary(lmm_observed),
               file = "taxonomic/lmm_observed_summary.txt")

# 5. Model estimated marginal means
write.csv(emm_shannon_df,
          "taxonomic/emmeans_shannon.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(emm_observed_df,
          "taxonomic/emmeans_observed.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# 6. Beta diversity objects
# distance matrix
saveRDS(
  psdist_tax,
  "taxonomic/aitchison_distance_genus_CLR.rds"
)

# metadata used in beta analysis
write.csv(
  meta_tax,
  "taxonomic/beta_metadata_genus_CLR.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# PERMANOVA longitudinal model
write.csv(
  as.data.frame(adonis_tax),
  "taxonomic/permanova_longitudinal_genus_CLR.csv"
)

# ordination scores (VERY useful later)
write.csv(
  scores_df,
  "taxonomic/pcoa_scores_genus_CLR.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# optional: eigenvalues
write.csv(
  data.frame(Variance = ord_tax$values$Relative_eig),
  "taxonomic/pcoa_variance_genus_CLR.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 7. Taxonomic abundance tables
write.csv(otu_tab,
          "taxonomic/otu_relative_abundance.csv")

write.csv(otu_long,
          "taxonomic/otu_relative_abundance_long.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(tax_tab,
          "taxonomic/taxonomy_table.csv")

write.csv(mean_abund_genus,
          "taxonomic/mean_genus_abundance.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(genus_table,
          "taxonomic/table_dominant_genera.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# 8. CLR abundance matrices
clr_all <- abundances(beta_tax)

write.csv(clr_all,
          "taxonomic/clr_genus_matrix.csv")

# 9. Final filtered phyloseq object
saveRDS(noneg_tax,
        "taxonomic/phyloseq_taxonomy_filtered.rds")

cat("✓ TAXONOMY reproducible outputs successfully saved\n")

```
